context.setupNotes: Build the first Lifelines HTML Design Lab slice. Keep scope narrow: phone practice two-attempt loop. Preserve seams between content, resolver, store, and UI. PM is the project backbone; Tycoon harness is not ported yet.
designDecisions: HTML asks whether the game loop is interesting; Godot later asks whether the existing sim can perform it.
Dice bias probability and readiness; they do not select deterministic authored branches.
Vignette beats are cheap stand-ins for scene behavior; Frank report interprets evidence but should not carry the primary payoff.

tasks.title: Scaffold React/Vite/MobX design lab
tasks.description: Create the new lab folder with PM config, package scripts, TypeScript/Vite setup, README, docs, and a clear architecture rule.
tasks.verification: npm run build and npm test can run from a clean checkout after npm install.

progressNotes.text: Scaffolded git repo, PM config, React/Vite/TypeScript/MobX package, README, docs/runs convention, and architecture sketch. npm test and npm run build pass.

tasks.title: Implement Phone Practice domain/content/resolver seam
tasks.description: Add typed domain concepts, authored phone-practice content, and a pure resolver for dice-biased outcomes, vignette beats, evidence, reports, and next approaches.
steps: Author Elling phone state object and approaches
tasks.verification: Resolver test proves same context repeats, better support improves readiness, and next approaches exist.

progressNotes.text: Added typed domain/content/resolver seam for phone practice: state object, approaches, dice-biased readiness/outcomes, vignette beats, evidence, Frank report, next approaches, and Vitest coverage. npm test passes.

tasks.title: Implement first playable Phone Practice Lab UI
tasks.description: Expose current state, apartment overlay, approach selection, die selection, vignette timeline, evidence, Frank report, and next-approach buttons.
steps: Style the lab for readable browser testing
tasks.verification: Manual browser run can execute at least two attempts with changed approach and visible result differences.

progressNotes.text: Implemented playable first UI: current state, apartment overlay, approach selector, die/Frank stance controls, vignette timeline, evidence/report, and next-approach buttons. Browser smoke on localhost:8791 produced attempt results with no console errors.

tasks.title: Run harness-lite adversarial review
tasks.description: Create a docs/runs trace for the first slice and review whether the two-attempt loop produces a better next decision.
tasks.verification: docs/runs contains goal, contract, trace, evaluator, and verdict for the first design run.
